topic.what: Vulnerability propagation in npm JavaScript dependency networks
topic.how: Large-scale analysis of 1,077,946 npm packages using OSV/NVD APIs and Kaplan-Meier survival analysis to model vulnerability discovery, publication, and remediation timelines
topic.why_matters: npm ecosystem underpins modern web development with 4M+ packages; 21.6% of packages affected by vulnerabilities with 42% high-severity and propagation ratio of 3.6x creates systemic security risk

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of npm packages with at least one vulnerability in dependency network
characteristics.evidence: 232,836 of 1,077,946 parent packages depend on vulnerable packages
characteristics.context: 21.6% of npm ecosystem affected by known vulnerabilities

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total number of packages affected by vulnerabilities in dataset
characteristics.evidence: Direct count from dataset of 1,077,946 packages
characteristics.context: Over 232K packages have at least one vulnerability in their dependency chain

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate of increase in published vulnerabilities over time
characteristics.evidence: Linear regression on accumulation data 2015-2024; linear model fits better than exponential
characteristics.context: Linear growth not exponential; suggests steady but manageable increase

characteristics.what_it_measures: Distribution of CVSS severity categories across 1,498 unique vulnerabilities
characteristics.evidence: CVSS v3.x analysis: 630 High, 394 Critical, 466 Medium, 8 Low
characteristics.context: 68% of vulnerabilities are High or Critical severity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time from first vulnerable version to fix publication
characteristics.context: Median time to fix is nearly 5 years after introduction

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of vulnerabilities where fix is published before public disclosure
characteristics.evidence: Fixes available before OSV/NVD publication for majority of CVEs
characteristics.context: Most fixes precede public disclosure by median 19 days

characteristics.what_it_measures: Concentration of vulnerabilities in top CVEs
characteristics.evidence: Top 7 most frequent CVEs account for 25% of all vulnerability instances; top 23 account for 50%
characteristics.context: Highly skewed distribution enables targeted remediation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average number of packages each affected package influences through dependency chains
characteristics.evidence: Each affected package influences average 3.6 other packages via dependencies
characteristics.context: Cascading vulnerability propagation through transitive dependencies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Typical depth of package dependency networks
characteristics.context: npm has highly interconnected dependency structure

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate of increase in packages affected by vulnerabilities over time
characteristics.evidence: Linear regression on affected package accumulation
characteristics.context: Linear growth in affected packages mirrors vulnerability growth
